fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Wisconsin Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Wisconsin - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity wisconsin, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to wisconsin, respiratory therapist compact wisconsin, wisconsin respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Wisconsin DSPS — Respiratory Care Practitioner
board.parent_dept: Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS)
board.mailing_address:
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services
P.O. Box 8368
Madison, WI 53708-8368

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) entry-level examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Wisconsin.

steps.title: Create a DSPS Online Licensing Portal Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services (DSPS) Online Licensing Portal if you do not already have one. You will need to provide personal details and set up login credentials.

steps.title: Start Your Application
steps.description: Log in to the DSPS Online Licensing Portal, select 'Start a New Application,' and choose 'Respiratory Care Practitioner' from the list of professions. Follow the prompts to complete all required sections of the application.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your respiratory care educational program and verification of your NBRC RRT credential to be sent directly to DSPS. Other supporting documents may be required depending on your application type.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required initial application fee of $150 through the DSPS Online Licensing Portal using a credit card or other accepted payment method.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check the status of your application within the DSPS Online Licensing Portal. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the DSPS.

steps.title: Receive Licensure
steps.description: Upon successful review and approval of your application and all supporting documentation, your Respiratory Care Practitioner license will be issued by the DSPS.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Wisconsin respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Wisconsin have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Wisconsin. To work in Wisconsin, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Wisconsin?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Wisconsin while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the DSPS Online Licensing Portal, click 'Register' for a new account, provide required personal information, create a username and password, and verify your email to activate the account.
